articles.title: PM Modi, Trump To Meet Today—What The Agenda Could Be
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump are set to meet on June 17 during the G7 Summit in France to address key issues including the situation in West Asia, energy imports from the US, and a bilateral trade agreement.

articles.title: TMC MLA Questions CID After Claiming Egg Attack Accused Entered Mamata's House As Witness
articles.description: A political controversy has erupted in West Bengal after TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh alleged that one of the individuals involved in the recent egg attack on him had earlier entered Mamata Banerjee's residence as an independent witness during a CID operation. Ghosh questioned how the alleged attacker was allowed access to the residence of the former Chief Minister, who is under Z-category security cover. He demanded answers on who recommended the individual to the CID and announced that he would formally write to the agency seeking clarification. The remarks have intensified the political row surrounding the attack and security arrangements outside Mamata Banerjee's residence.

articles.title: In Mumbai, a white line between ahimsa and anger
articles.description: Communal tension Mumbai: Dispute over a white painted pathway in Mumbai housing societies has triggered tensions between Jain community members and local residents over use of common spaces, due process, and rising concerns over communal harmony now

articles.description: According to sources, both nations are keen to deepen their energy partnership, with discussions likely to focus on increasing energy cooperation and ensuring stable supplies.

articles.title: The Hormuz secret Trump isn’t talking about: How long will IRGC mines keep trade hostage?
articles.description: The Strait of Hormuz may reopen soon after the expected U.S.-Iran peace agreement, but experts warn that a full return to normal shipping could take weeks. Concerns remain over the possible presence of naval mines, with specialists estimating that clearing key sea lanes and establishing safe navigation corridors may require 40 to 50 days. Nearly 20% of global energy supplies transit through Hormuz, making any disruption a major risk for oil markets and global trade. Britain, France, and Germany have already prepared warships and minesweepers for potential clearance operations, while shipping companies and insurers continue to monitor security conditions closely.

articles.title: Savarkar Filed 10 Mercy Petitions Before British, Grandnephew Tells Pune Court In Rahul Gandhi Defamation Trial
articles.description: Satyaki Savarkar contended that filing clemency petitions for sentence reduction was a standard administrative procedure under the British framework

articles.title: Iran Can Now Shut Down Strait of Hormuz Anytime, US Intel Raises Alarm | Watch
articles.description: A stunning U.S. intelligence assessment has shattered long-held assumptions about American naval dominance in the Middle East. Classified findings revealed by senior national security sources conclude that Iran now possesses the definitive capability to completely shut down the Strait of Hormuz—the world's most vital energy corridor. This shifting balance of power has forced a raw strategic reality on Washington, with Pentagon planners acknowledging that attempting to forcibly secure the narrow chokepoint carries catastrophic risks. As both sides prepare for the Geneva signing ceremony, Tehran is framing this moment domestically as proof that its low-cost, asymmetric coastal defense grid has successfully forced the Trump administration to negotiate on equal terms.

articles.title: DH Toon | Too obscene for a progressive society
Trump’s fear exposed in 24-hour frenzy: Iran deal done, third strike canceled, Israel told to...?
articles.description: Just days after a devastating conflict that brought the Middle East to the brink, Donald Trump is now making a stunning claim — that Iran has a “more rational” leadership. But what does that actually mean, and what happened to the figures Trump once described as the main obstacle to peace? After U.S. and Israeli strikes killed several top Iranian officials during the war, Trump suggested that a new political reality had emerged inside Tehran. He argued that the leaders he viewed as “totally irrational” were now gone and that Iran’s current leadership was more prepared to engage diplomatically with Washington. The comments come at a crucial moment as the U.S. and Iran attempt to preserve a fragile agreement designed to prevent Tehran from obtaining nuclear weapons and avoid another regional war. However, Trump’s remarks have also sparked questions over whether military pressure genuinely changed Iran’s strategic calculations or whether the peace remains vulnerable to future disagreements. At the same time, the deal faces opposition from hardliners in both Washington and Tehran, while Israel continues to raise concerns about Iran’s long-term intentions. Is Trump witnessing a real transformation inside Iran, or is this merely a temporary pause before the next crisis in the Middle East?

articles.title: Putin watching this awkward G7 video? Trump gives Zelensky the cold shoulder while closing Iran deal
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump appeard to visibly snub Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the G7, even as leaders publicly pledged continued support for Kyiv. Trump's focus on finalizing an Iran deal and his dismissive “this whole thing is ridiculous” remark have fueled questions over whether Washington’s commitment to Ukraine is wavering despite Zelensky’s insistence that G7 backing remains “unanimous.”

articles.title: On Cam: Zelensky Ignored By Trump In Huge G7 Embarrassment; ‘Painful’ Moment Viral
articles.description: An awkward moment involving Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelensky is making waves online after cameras captured the two leaders ahead of crucial Ukraine talks at the G7 Summit in France. Footage appeared to show Trump turning away as he greeted other leaders, fueling fresh speculation about tensions between Washington and Kyiv. The viral clip comes as world leaders debate the future of the Ukraine war and efforts to push Russia toward negotiations.

articles.title: Todd Blanche set for July confirmation hearings for attorney general as Republicans weigh support
articles.description: WASHINGTON — The Senate Judiciary Committee has scheduled confirmation hearings for acting Attorney General Todd Blanche in mid-July, even as some Republicans remain undecided about whether to support him for the post. President Donald Trump nominated Blanche to lead the Justice Department this month, shortly after Blanche sparred with Republican senators ...

articles.title: Ascent through allies: The BJP playbook
articles.description: Regional Parties: BJP's use of alliances and splits has shaped Indian politics, weakening several regional parties while expanding its national footprint through strategic coalitions, state-level partnerships and long-term organisational planning. .

articles.title: AWKWARD! Trump Left Standing Alone As G7 Leaders Huddle And Laugh In Brutal Viral Photo
articles.description: A single photo from the G7 Summit in France is triggering a wave of reactions online. As world leaders gathered in a tight huddle ahead of crucial talks on Iran and Ukraine, US President Donald Trump appeared to be standing apart from the group. The image quickly went viral, with critics portraying it as a symbol of Trump's isolation on the world stage. The awkward optics come as leaders seek consensus on a new Iran peace deal and the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war. But before the high-stakes discussions even began, one snapshot had already become the defining image of the summit.

articles.title: Proverb of the Day: 'A tent without a woman is like...' Life lessons on harmony, happiness, sanctuary, emotional connection, mutual support, and why a female's presence is essential to make a home livable
articles.description: Proverb of the Day highlights the saying, “A tent without a woman is like a violin with no strings.” The proverb explains the value of emotional connection, harmony, mutual support, and family life. It compares a home without emotional care to a violin that cannot create music. The teaching remains relevant today as people continue to build relationships through understanding, cooperation, and shared responsibilities.

Jayarajan aboard an IndiGo flight during a protest against former Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan four years ago.

articles.title: Maharana Pratap Jayanti: CM Sharma extends greetings, hails his ideals
articles.description: Rajasthan CM Bhajan Lal Sharma extended greetings on Maharana Pratap Jayanti, describing him as a symbol of supreme self-respect. He urged citizens to imbibe the iconic ruler's ideals and dedicate themselves to the service of the nation.

articles.title: Can't give relaxation in 30-day notice period for marriage under Special Marriage Act: Delhi High Court
articles.description: Delhi High Court: The Delhi High Court rejected a plea to waive the 30-day notice period under the Special Marriage Act. The court said personal hardship cannot override statutory requirements, and marriages must follow the legal waiting period.

articles.title: BJP attacks Bhagwant Mann after Akal Takht declares him 'anti-Guru'
articles.description: BJP's Shehzad Poonawalla attacked Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann after the Akal Takht reportedly declared him 'anti-Guru'. Poonawalla cited a viral video, alleging Mann insulted Sikh sentiments and called for his resignation on moral grounds.

articles.title: Congress hits back at DMK, tells it to self-introspect, not mock Rahul
articles.description: Congress MP Imran Masood slammed the DMK for mocking Rahul Gandhi, telling them to 'save its own existence'. This follows a DMK mouthpiece article criticising Rahul's 'political immaturity' and Congress undermining INDIA bloc allies in state polls.

articles.title: Trump to release Iran deal text, will read it 'word by word' to media
articles.description: Donald Trump has vowed to release the text of the Iran agreement in a few days, even suggesting he'd read it live to the media. He said the deal's core is to prevent Iran from getting a nuclear weapon and has now agreed to send it to Congress.

articles.title: Saugata Roy accuses Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar of leading TMC rebellion
articles.description: TMC MP Saugata Roy accused Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar of leading a 20-MP rebellion against Mamata Banerjee. He cited internal conflicts in the group, which has asked the Speaker to merge with the NCPI, a move slammed by other TMC leaders.

articles.title: Priyanka Gandhi on paper leaks: It's a deep systematic problem
articles.description: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra expressed solidarity with students over repeated paper leaks, calling it a systemic issue. Rahul Gandhi will lead a series of student conventions in Kota, Allahabad, Patna, and Delhi to address examination scandals.

articles.title: Puri servitors honour Odisha CM for removing 'Dham' from Digha temple
articles.description: Jagannath Temple servitors in Puri held a civic reception for Odisha CM Mohan Charan Majhi to thank him for getting the word 'Dham' removed from a temple in Digha, West Bengal. Devotees consider Puri the only true Jagannath Dham.

articles.title: Amid 'Operation Tiger' buzz, UBT Sena denies MPs joining Shinde camp
articles.description: Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs Anil Desai and Sanjay Raut dismissed speculation of MPs joining the Eknath Shinde-led faction. They asserted that all leaders continue to have faith in Uddhav Thackeray's leadership, calling the defection rumors baseless.

articles.title: Punjab CM Responds to Akal Takht Verdict, Denies Link to Viral Video
articles.description: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann has responded to the Akal Takht's decision declaring him 'Guru Dokhi' and 'Khalsa Panth Virodhi', rejecting allegations linked to a controversial viral video. Mann said he had already appeared before the Akal Takht and clarified that the person seen in the footage was not him. While expressing disappointment over the verdict, he reiterated his respect for the Akal Takht and alleged that a political campaign was being carried out against him under the guise of religion.

articles.title: G7 discusses Ukraine, China; Japan proposes critical minerals plan
articles.description: At the G7 summit, Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi said leaders discussed Ukraine, West Asia, and China. She proposed a 'Joint Stockpiling Collaboration Initiative' for critical minerals and addressed issues like Iran's nuclear program and North Korea.

Video of Trump tightly holding Brigitte's hand and appearing to engage in a handshake tug-of-war sparked a flood of reactions on social media. Here's what happened during the uncomfortable G7 greeting and why the internet can't stop talking about it.

articles.title: US President Donald Trump May Skip US-Iran Peace Deal Signing, JD Vance Attendance Confirm | News18
articles.description: | President Donald Trump has indicated that he may not attend the formal signing ceremony of the US–Iran peace agreement in Switzerland, despite playing a central role in brokering the deal. Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 Summit, Trump said Vice President JD Vance would "definitely" be present at the Geneva ceremony, while leaving his own participation uncertain. Vance has emerged as a key figure in the negotiations and has maintained that the agreement serves American interests by reopening the Strait of Hormuz and preventing Iran from pursuing nuclear weapons. n18oc_breaking-news News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: 'Dham' removed from Digha's Jagannath Temple, Patra hails WB LoP
articles.description: BJP MP Sambit Patra lauded West Bengal LoP Suvendu Adhikari for removing 'Dham' from Digha's Jagannath Temple name. The move, a long-standing demand by devotees who consider Puri the sole 'Jagannath Dham', was widely celebrated in Odisha.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari visits KMC, pays respects to Netaji, plans overhaul
articles.description: Suvendu Adhikari visited the KMC office, announcing a major delimitation process to address ward voter disparities. He also launched a five-day cleanliness drive in Kolkata to prepare for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's upcoming visit.
